title: Arrive on Time
summary: Plan when to leave so you are less likely to feel rushed.
learnerPromise: Plan when to leave so you are less likely to feel rushed.
whyItMatters: Being on time shows respect and reliability. It also reduces your own stress and gives you a calmer start.
realLifeUses: Get to school or work on time.
Catch a bus, train, or flight without panic.
Meet friends or colleagues when you said you would.
youWillLearn: Build buffer time into your plan.
miniChallenge: Pick one upcoming event and calculate what time you need to leave with a 10-minute buffer.
steps: Estimate how long it takes to get there. Add fifteen minutes for delays.
Prepare everything you need the night before.
Set an alarm to start getting ready, not just to leave.
Check traffic or transit schedules before you go.
Leave when your plan says leave, not when you feel ready.
completionCriteria: You arrive early to one appointment.
You can estimate travel time for a new destination.
commonProblems: Not accounting for traffic, weather, or transit delays.
tips: Set two alarms: one to start preparing, one to leave.
Pack your bag and choose clothes the night before.
Build in a buffer so small delays do not make you late.
